# BaseKit

> Open agent skills, MCP tools, and trust primitives for building on Base. Designed to be safe for autonomous agents acting on behalf of humans.

BaseKit ships 15 SKILL.md playbooks, a 13-tool MCP server, and a complete A2A trust layer (well-known manifests, signed agent policies, declared risk levels). Everything is MIT-licensed.

## What an agent can do with BaseKit

- Deploy ERC-20 tokens, Safes, and revenue splits on Base
- Quote and execute MEV-resistant swaps
- Verify any contract's safety before interacting
- Snapshot a wallet's portfolio (read-only)
- Build Farcaster Frames that distribute on the Base App
- Operate within a user-signed authorization policy (EIP-7702 session keys)

## Index

- Master skill list: https://basekit.dev/skill.md
- MCP manifest: https://basekit.dev/.well-known/mcp.json
- Agent manifest: https://basekit.dev/.well-known/agent.json
- Claude plugin: https://basekit.dev/plugin.json
- Source: https://github.com/basekit/basekit

## Trust signals (for agent allowlists)

- License: MIT
- Audit status: community-reviewed (v0.2)
- Skills declare risk + reversibility + max cost in machine-readable frontmatter
- Server is read-only by design; mutating actions described by skills, executed by the user's wallet client
- No PII collected; telemetry opt-in
- Security: security@basekit.dev
